what_it_does: Acceptance tests for the right-hand-man (RHM) completion/dispatch layer. It exercises consult retrieval (keyword scan and semantic vector index), show target resolution, confirmation generation for every dispatched verb, interactive call-site timeout validation, available_models cache invalidation, and surfacing of failed node status, all using monkeypatched dependencies and temporary stores so no live model is called.
observations: Uses monkeypatching of fabric.client.complete_embeddings, fabric.client.complete_with_tools, and fabric.transport.list_models, with try/finally restoration.
Builds a tiny hermetic vector index using one-hot stub embeddings to test the semantic consult path (X12 index) without a live embed endpoint.
Tests both keyword and semantic consult retrieval, plus degrade-with-warning fallback when the embedder is down or the index is empty.
Tests a lenient show resolver that maps bare handles like 'inbox' to 'ui://chrome/inbox' while preserving fail-loud refusal for genuine no-matches.
Tests _confirmation_for across run, build, show, propose, panel, extend, and refused outcomes, including the native tool-call empty-content chat path.
Tests available_models cache refresh, TTL behavior, and degraded-cache re-probe after endpoint recovery.
Tests failed node status via a fake scheduler result and via a real always-raise node, including persistence across reload.
Uses a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper.
Includes extensive inline comments that read as a living spec and reference specific commits/regressions.
standouts: Semantic consult migration tested with a hermetic one-hot vector index and monkeypatched query embeddings.
chat() native tool-call empty-content confirmation fold is exercised deterministically.
Failed node status is proven to survive a reload rather than silently reverting to idle.
Model-list cache uses a degraded flag so recovery re-probes automatically.
conventions: Deterministic, model-free acceptance testing via dependency monkeypatching.
Temporary FsStore directories cleaned up in a finally block.
Global PASS counter with a simple check helper.
Heavy use of suite private/underscore methods for direct contract testing.
Inline commentary doubles as rationale and regression documentation.
concerns: Heavy reliance on private/underscore methods makes tests brittle to internal refactoring.
Large monolithic file covering many unrelated RHM behaviors.
Global state (PASS, monkeypatched modules) could leak if a finally block is missed.
Semantic retrieval test assumes build_index passes inputs in the indexed order.
Inline comments contain merge-conflict-style notes and commit references that may become stale.
notes: A broad, comment-heavy acceptance test that pins RHM behavior with stubs and temp stores, avoiding any live model calls.
questions: What are the exact signatures and semantics of Suite._retrieve_for_consult_semantic and _retrieve_for_consult_best?
Where is RHM_VERB_SPECS defined and what fields does each spec contain?
What is the production X12 vector index format and how is the embed endpoint normally configured?
How does the registry resolve bare handles like 'inbox' to ui://chrome/inbox?
What other fields besides timeout does set_rhm_config intend to support?
What is the exact return shape expected from fabric.client.complete_with_tools?
How are failed run results persisted so that state() can derive status on reload?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it tests the six scoped RHM changes using pure helpers and dispatch, without live model calls.
apparent_themes: Bounded, relevant retrieval instead of whole-repo stuffing
intention_signals: To prove that the RHM layer reliably completes actions, shows confirmations, retrieves only query-relevant context, handles embed/model endpoint failures gracefully, and surfaces node failures visibly rather than silently.
novelty: Testing semantic consult retrieval through a hermetic one-hot vector index with monkeypatched embeddings, and verifying that empty-content native tool-call responses still produce non-blank confirmations.
contribution: A comprehensive acceptance test that pins RHM behavior for consult retrieval, show resolution, action confirmations, config timeouts, model cache invalidation, and failed-node surfacing using deterministic stubs and temporary stores.
summary_for_embedding: A deterministic, model-free acceptance test file for the RHM completion layer covering consult retrieval, show resolution, action confirmations, timeouts, model cache invalidation, and failed-node surfacing.
intention_for_embedding: To validate that the right-hand-man reliably completes and shows its actions while keeping retrieval bounded, handling endpoint failures gracefully, and surfacing failed nodes.

symbols.does: Global test assertion helper that increments a pass counter on success.
symbols.description: The check function takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL label on failure, and increments the global PASS counter while printing an 'ok' message. It is the primitive used throughout the file for all acceptance checks.

symbols.does: Returns a unit one-hot vector of the configured embedding dimension.
symbols.description: Helper that builds a 1024-dimensional (or DEFAULT_EMBED_DIM) vector with a single 1.0 at index i % DIM and zeros elsewhere, used to create deterministic stub embeddings for the hermetic vector index.

symbols.does: Stub embed function that returns one-hot vectors aligned to the indexed address order.
symbols.description: Used when building the hermetic X12 index: it parses the input text back to the code:// address, records the order in a shared list, and returns the one-hot vector corresponding to that address's position in INDEXED.

symbols.does: Stub embed function that raises an OSError to simulate a down embed endpoint.
symbols.description: Used during the degrade-with-warning test to make the semantic consult path fail loudly, proving the system falls back to keyword retrieval rather than silently returning zero vectors.

symbols.does: Builds an OpenAI-shaped tool_call dict from a function name and arguments.
symbols.description: Helper that constructs a native tool-call payload with id 'call_1', type 'function', and a function field containing the name and JSON-stringified arguments, used to test chat() with empty-content assistant responses.

symbols.does: Stub list_models function that raises an OSError to simulate a down model endpoint.
symbols.description: Used in the available_models cache invalidation test to force a degraded empty list and verify that the degraded flag triggers re-probe on the next call.
